# Isolation Checklist: Embedded TikTok Analytics In Member Hub

**Purpose**: Review requirements quality for host isolation, non-interference, and safe in-platform containment before implementation  
**Created**: 2026-05-13  
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/miguelcrasto/development/tiktokanalytics/specs/001-member-hub-embed/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are host-isolation requirements specified separately for navigation integrity, layout integrity, and unrelated member-hub controls instead of as one broad "does not break" statement? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK002 Are regression-protection requirements defined for both non-app materials behavior and at least one unrelated member-hub flow? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK003 Does the spec define host-owned requirements for blocked framing, service outage, and partially blocked embed content? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK004 Are diagnostics or observability requirements documented for embed load failures, frame-policy regressions, and degraded host states? [Gap, Constitution §Principle 4, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK005 Are release-gating requirements defined for when the embedded tool entry may become user-visible in the host? [Gap, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §Host Integration State]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "surrounding platform context" defined with concrete expectations about preserved route, breadcrumb, and page usability? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "does not break or alter unrelated member hub behavior" decomposed into measurable host surfaces or behaviors? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "safe fallback experience" defined with required user-facing actions and messaging elements, rather than only intent language? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "partially rolled out integration" described with concrete failure conditions and acceptance boundaries? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Summary]

## Requirement Consistency & Acceptance Quality

- [ ] CHK010 Are FR-002 and FR-006 clearly distinguished so one owns full in-platform completion and the other owns contained internal navigation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK011 Do FR-008 and FR-009 align on rollout coordination, backward compatibility, and user-exposure timing without contradictory release expectations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK012 Can SC-003 be evaluated objectively without guessing which "non-analyzer member hub flow" must remain intact? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK013 Can SC-004 be evaluated objectively across outage, framing restriction, and partial-content failure scenarios? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Scenario & Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are requirements complete for the leave-and-return journey so host-state preservation expectations are defined both when exiting the tool and when re-entering it? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK015 Are coexistence requirements defined for smaller viewports where the host shell and embedded surface share limited space? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK016 Are requirements defined for the case where the iframe shell appears but internal analytics content or navigation becomes blocked? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Embedded Analytics Session]
- [ ] CHK017 Are requirements explicit about what must remain available in the member hub while the embedded tool is degraded? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-007]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK018 Are cross-repo dependency requirements documented for host schema changes, framing policy control, deploy order, and rollback ownership? [Dependencies, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the standalone-versus-embed boundary specified clearly enough to prevent host-specific behavior or theming from leaking into the standalone analytics surface? [Consistency, Plan §Summary, Data Model §Embed Presentation Profile]
- [ ] CHK020 Are ownership assumptions documented for who approves host-regression risk, framing-policy changes, and rollback decisions across the two repositories? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Project Structure]

## Notes

- Use this checklist as a pre-implementation requirements review for isolation and non-interference.
- Mark findings inline if any item reveals a gap, ambiguity, or conflict that should be resolved before coding.
